questions.question_summary: Select the correct numeric answer from the given multiple‑choice options (13, 15, 17, 19).
Identify the primary role of self‑attention in transformer models by choosing the correct description from the options provided.
State the primary … (the question is incomplete in the source text).
Explain the role and purpose of the attention mechanism in a transformer network.
Identify the primary architectural difference between a gated recurrent unit (GRU) and an LSTM cell.
State what the multi‑head self‑attention mechanism enables in transformer models.
Calculate the total number of trainable parameters in a fully connected neural network with 2 inputs, 3 hidden units, and 2 outputs.
Describe the main objective of a generative adversarial network (GAN).
Choose the most appropriate loss function from the given options (MSE, RMSE, Cross‑Entropy, Cosine Similarity) for the described scenario.
Determine which types of features (node only, edge only, both, or neither) are used in the given graph‑based model.
Explain the primary purpose of the bottleneck layer in an autoencoder network.
Identify which types of information can be included as (incomplete prompt).
Identify features in Graph Neural Networks that enhance their learning capabilities
Define what the term “stride” refers to in Convolutional Neural Networks
Explain the primary effect or purpose of using stride in a CNN (e.g., speed, compression, overfitting reduction, etc.)
Select the loss function that is commonly used for training classification models in deep learning
Determine the correct output tensor shape after applying a VALID convolution with stride 1 and no padding, given specific input and filter dimensions
Identify which class/category achieved the highest prediction accuracy and state the percentage of correct predictions for that class
Describe how samples are distributed across categories, comment on each category, and assess whether the dataset is balanced
Calculate the overall accuracy of a three‑class model using the provided confusion matrix.
Explain why a confusion matrix provides more insight than a single overall accuracy figure for a classification problem.
Derive a closed‑form expression for the gradient of the specified function (likely a loss) with respect to its parameters.
Find the closed‑form expression for the gradient of the weight‑update rule of a single neuron that models a binary outcome.
Predict the output that will be produced by the given sequence of commands.
Determine the output shape (or error) when applying a fully connected nn.Linear layer with in_features=16 and out_features=8 to tensors of shapes [30, 16] and [30, 16, 32, 32].
Compute the output shape (or error) of a nn.Conv2d layer with in_channels=16, out_channels=40, kernel_size=8, padding=2 when applied to a tensor of shape [30, 16, 32, 32].
Compute the output shape (or error) of a nn.Conv2d layer with in_channels=16, out_channels=32, kernel_size=16, stride=4, padding=0 when applied to a tensor of shape [30, 16, 32, 32].
Determine the output shape (or error) of a nn.MaxPool2d layer with kernel_size=16, stride=3, padding=4 when applied to a tensor of shape [30, 16, 32, 32].

Compute the output shape (or error) of a nn.ConvTranspose2d layer with in_channels=16, out_channels=8, kernel_size=3, stride=?, padding=1, output_padding=1 when applied to a tensor of shape [30, 16, 32, 32].

Describe the end‑to‑end training pipeline for a GRU‑based text generation model, covering data preparation, model training steps, the role of the loss function, and how to generate new text with the trained model.
Fill in missing code to define a GRU-based text generation model in PyTorch, including initialization of vocabulary size, hidden dimension, GRU layer, and softmax output.
Explain the functions of the Query, Key, and Value matrices within the attention mechanism of a Transformer network.
Given matrices Q, K, and V, describe how to compute the attention output using the softmax operation.
Explain how the self‑attention mechanism in a Transformer contributes to the neural‑dictionary concept by relating input elements to each other.
Explain the purpose of scaling the dot-product in attention mechanisms and state the final output tensor shape of the scaled dot-product attention operation.

Describe the overall architecture of a graph neural network used to predict toxicity of chemical compounds, including key components, how the graph (atoms and bonds) is processed, and how a binary classification output is produced.

Propose a modification to the GNN architecture that enables it to capture global structural information of a chemical compound, and discuss the benefits and potential challenges of adding this global‑feature component.
Outline the main components of a generative adversarial network, detailing the roles of the generator and discriminator and describing how the two networks interact during training.
Explain what mode collapse is in generative adversarial networks, describe how it affects the quality of generated samples, and propose one technique to mitigate it.
Define the loss function used for the generator and the loss function used for the discriminator in a GAN.

questions.topic: Common Architectural Elements in Deep Learning Models
questions.question_summary: Identify three typical features or architectural components that appear in most deep learning models (e.g., layers, activation functions, regularization) and briefly explain their roles and how they enhance learning capability.
Discuss the various challenges that deep learning models encounter during the training process.

Compare and contrast the training processes of Convolutional Neural Networks and Transformer networks, providing at least two differences (e.g., architecture, model type, dataset requirements) and one similarity in the training steps.
Identify and briefly explain three common challenges encountered when training deep neural networks, regardless of the specific model architecture.
